name: Flournoy v. First Nat. Bank of Shreveport
summary: Louisiana Supreme Court decision addressing property interests, banking regulations, and liberty interests in commercial transactions.
holding: Property rights and liberty interests in commercial relationships protected; government restrictions must have rational basis and not arbitrarily interfere with contractual freedom.
constitutional_basis: Fourteenth Amendment Due Process, Contract Clause
relevance_to_audit: Relevant to analysis of government interference with property rights and commercial relationships; applicable to vendor contract restrictions and regulatory burdens.
